sections.subtitle: Match Overview and Context
sections.text: Osasuna, currently sitting at 9th in the league with 44 points, hosts Sevilla, who are struggling in 16th place with 38 points, in Round 33 at the Estadio El Sadar in Pamplona. This matchup presents a crucial opportunity for Osasuna to capitalize on their mid-table stability against a Sevilla side desperate to climb out of the lower ranks. The stakes are high as both teams look to improve their standings with just a few rounds remaining in the season.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two teams, Osasuna has secured 2 wins, while 3 encounters ended in draws, with Sevilla yet to claim a victory. Their most recent clash on December 2, 2024, ended in a 1-1 draw, where Sevilla's Dodi Lukebakio earned the Player of the Match title with a Sofascore rating of 7.8. Head-to-head trends also suggest tight games, with 8 out of the last 10 matches featuring under 2.5 goals.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Osasuna enters this match with a positive streak of no losses in their last 4 games, though they’ve failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 3 home matches. Sevilla, on the other hand, is in poor form, winless in their last 5 away games and without a clean sheet in the same span. Additionally, Osasuna’s games have seen over 2.5 goals in 5 of their last 7 matches, while Sevilla’s recent fixtures have mostly stayed under 2.5 goals in 6 of their last 8 outings.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Osasuna
sections.text: Osasuna’s attack will heavily rely on Ante Budimir, a forward ranked 3rd in the league for goals scored with 18, surpassing his expected goals of 16.65. Budimir has also been in form, netting 3 goals in his last 5 matches with a Sofascore rating of 7.08. Midfielder Rubén García adds creativity with 5 goals and 5 assists, while defender Alejandro Catena stands out defensively, ranking 3rd in the league for percentage of long balls in total passes at 9%.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Sevilla
sections.text: Sevilla’s offensive hopes lie with Dodi Lukebakio, who has 11 goals this season and ranks 3rd in the league for successful dribbles per game at 2.4. Midfielder Saúl Ñíguez contributes with 6 assists and a solid Sofascore rating of 7.0, while Juanlu Sánchez offers balance in midfield with 4 goals and 4 assists. These players will be critical if Sevilla are to break their winless streak.

sections.subtitle: Standout Performers from Past Encounters
sections.text: Osasuna’s goalkeeper Sergio Herrera has been a consistent performer against Sevilla in the last 5 matchups, boasting an average Sofascore rating of 7.36 with 4 saves per game across 5 starts. His presence between the posts could be a deciding factor in this game. Meanwhile, Sevilla will hope for another standout performance from Dodi Lukebakio, who shone in their last meeting.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Statistics
sections.text: Osasuna ranks 8th in the league for total goals scored with 42, though their defense has conceded 50 goals, placing them 14th for least goals conceded. They excel in aerial duels, ranking 2nd with a 54.98% win rate, and in accurate crosses, also ranking 2nd with 185. Sevilla’s struggles are evident with no standout season stats provided, but their inability to keep clean sheets in recent games (5 in a row) could be exploited by Osasuna’s attack.

sections.subtitle: Disciplinary and Set-Piece Expectations
sections.text: With referee Adrian Cordero Vega officiating, who averages 5.2 yellow cards per game, expect a potentially feisty encounter. Osasuna has accumulated 82 yellow cards this season (13th fewest in the league), averaging 2.41 per game, while Sevilla’s away games have seen over 4.5 cards in 4 of their last 5 matches. In terms of set-pieces, Osasuna averages 4.35 corners per game (13th in the league), and both teams have a trend of under 10.5 corners in recent matches (7 of 9 for Osasuna, 5 of 6 for Sevilla).

sections.subtitle: Interesting Facts and Trivia
sections.text: Osasuna has not lost to Sevilla in their last 7 head-to-head meetings, a streak they’ll aim to extend at home. Sevilla’s goal distribution shows they are strongest late in games, with 21.74% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes. Meanwhile, Osasuna has a knack for scoring early in the second half, with 10 goals between the 46th and 60th minutes. Additionally, Osasuna ranks 1st in the league for the percentage of big chances converted at 47.17%, a stat that could prove decisive.
